plain_definition: Representing ownership of a real-world asset -- such as a government bond, a fund, or a bank deposit -- as a digital token on a blockchain-style ledger, so that it can be transferred, settled, or traded the way virtual assets are, rather than through traditional paper-based or centralised-registry processes. Hong Kong's Policy Statement 2.0 names expanding tokenised products, and reviewing the legal treatment of tokenised real-world assets (RWA), as core planks of its digital-asset strategy.
formal_definition: The process of issuing a digital token that represents rights to an underlying real-world asset (RWA); referenced in Policy Statement 2.0 on the Development of Digital Assets in Hong Kong as a strategic priority pursued through initiatives such as Project Ensemble and tokenised government bond issuances.
